items.title: Iraqi national pleads not guilty in 18 attacks in Europe, calling himself 'prisoner of war'
items.summary: “I'm not guilty in a war situation, I'm a prisoner of war. I'm not a threat. Children and women are being killed by your rockets,” says Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi

items.title: Former Sri Lanka President Gotabaya Rajapaksa allowed to testify online on rights activists' disappearance hearing
items.summary: At the time of the right activists' disappearance, Mr. Rajapaksa was accused of overseeing abduction squads that whisked away rebel suspects, critical journalists, and activists, many of them never to be seen again

items.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 16, traps others in damaged buildings
items.summary: Ukraine’s Foreign Minister said the latest Russian attack was a sign of Russian desperation, that President Vladimir Putin was bombing civilians since his forces were struggling on the battlefield

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Iran has not yet sent its response to the U.S. in talks, says report
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to stop fighting, adding that Iran talks were moving rapidly after seeming to falter over Israel’s Lebanon offensive

items.title: Pakistan: Army, religion, and the enemy to the east
items.summary: The e-book brings together the expertise of leading journalists and scholars to decode the region’s dangerous “new normal”.

items.title: Congo re-opens airport at centre of Ebola outbreak
items.summary: Democratic Republic of the Congo has reopened Bunia airport in Ituri province, the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak, as authorities seek to restore access to critical supplies while intensifying efforts to contain the disease

items.title: UN warns of extreme heat risk from El Nino, urges preparedness
items.summary: The WMO has warned that a developing El Niño could drive higher global temperatures and increase the risk of heatwaves, droughts and heavy rainfall in the coming months

items.title: Over 550 Indian peacekeepers in South Sudan awarded U.N. Medal of Honour
items.summary: The peacekeepers were recognised for their efforts in ‘protecting civilians through patrols, community engagement, veterinary camps, women's self-defence training, combating gender violence and improving humanitarian access’

items.title: Lebanon's U.S. Embassy says Hezbollah accepted U.S. proposal on 'mutual cessation of attacks'
items.summary: The Lebanese authorities received confirmation of Hezbollah's acceptance of the U.S. proposal providing for a mutual cessation of attacks, an Embassy statement published by Lebanese President Joseph Aoun's office said.

items.title: Canada is failing Jewish community and Jews are being targeted: PM Carney
items.summary: The Prime Minister said that across Canada, antisemitism has surged to levels not seen in the post-World War II era

items.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariff to punish Brazil over trade practices

items.title: U.S. in talks to expand nuclear weapons deployments in Europe: report
items.summary: The move would ‌involve ⁠more countries to host so-called ⁠U.S. dual-capable aircraft which are able to deliver nuclear strikes

items.title: Tropical storm Jangmi injures 9 in Japan, thousands without power
items.summary: The Japan Meteorological Agency warned of high waves, landslides and flooding as storm Jangmi rumbles northwards after bearing down on the subtropical island of Okinawa

items.title: Gunman kills six family members, then himself, in eastern Iowa, police say
items.summary: Four of ⁠the victims were found shot to death inside a residence by police officers responding to the first reports of gunfire shortly after noon local ‌time

items.title: Pakistan sees 27% increase in terror attacks in May: Report
items.summary: The report also showed a sharp increase in suicide attacks as the country witnessed six suicide attacks during May

items.title: Cambodia begins UN process to resolve maritime dispute with Thailand
items.summary: The move by Cambodia to begin the mediation process under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, followed Thailand’s cancellation last month of a 2001 bilateral agreement on overlapping maritime claims and energy exploration.

items.title: Beaufort Castle captured by Israel: Why is the Lebanon fortress important?
items.summary: The Hezbollah troops said that their fighters were still actively battling Israeli troops near Beaufort Castle, describing the situation as a “battle of attrition.”

items.title: Oil prices fall amid Trump’s talks with Iran
items.summary: Brent crude futures lost 75 cents, or 0.79%, to $94.23 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell 85 ‌cents, or 0.92%, to $91.31 a barrel

items.title: Israel launches strikes on Lebanon’s Beirut after Hezbollah fires missiles to enter West Asia conflict
items.summary: Hezbollah said its strikes were carried out in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, and for “repeated Israeli aggressions”

items.title: As Trump cheerleads for AI, some in MAGA world fret

items.title: 30 Indians living and working illegally in U.S. as truck drivers arrested, will be deported
items.summary: Operation Checkmate is aimed at enhancing public safety through enforcement of immigration statutes to detect and arrest illegal persons operating commercial motor vehicles

items.title: U.S. court blocks Pentagon from removing transgender troops, for now
items.summary: A panel ⁠of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit in a 2-1 ruling said the 2025 policy was unlawfully motivated “by the bare desire to harm a politically unpopular group.”

items.title: Trump administration agrees to temporarily freeze 'slush fund' for allies
items.summary: U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema barred the administration last week from taking any further action to create or operate the fund ahead of a June 12 court hearing.

items.title: Iran suspends talks with U.S. over Israel’s Lebanon strikes
items.summary: If the Houthis, Iran’s allies in Yemen, open a new front in the conflict, one obvious target would be ​the Bab El Mandeb ‌Strait off the coast of Yemen, a key shipping chokepoint and narrow passageway that controls sea traffic towards the Suez Canal

items.title: Myanmar not to allow use of its territory by anti-India elements: President Min Aung Hlaing
items.summary: India to train Myanmar military for UN peacekeeping; discussions between the two countries include the issue of continued detention of former leader and Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi as well as infrastructure projects such as the Kaladan Multimodal Transit Transport Project and the trilateral Moreh-Mae Tsot highway to connect India’s northeast with Myanmar via Thailand

items.title: U.K. blocks visit by two left-wing U.S. political commentators
items.summary: The U.K. Home Office confirmed to The Hindu that the travel authorisations for both individuals were cancelled because their presence would not be conducive to the public good

items.title: Ebola recoveries bring signs of hope in Congo as suspected cases emerge outside Africa
items.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said ​that although there is no licensed vaccine or treatment for Ebola caused by the Bundibugyo virus currently, it ⁠can be survived with good medical care

items.title: Watch: Iran suspends talks as Israel widens Lebanon offensive | Above the Fold | 01.06.2026

items.title: Russian strikes kill one, wound two dozen in Ukraine
items.summary: The Ukrainian air force said Russian forces had launched 265 combat drones in the barrage, and that its air defence units had downed 228 of the unmanned aerial vehicles

items.title: India, Australia discuss maritime security, defence cooperation
items.summary: India and Australia further reaffirmed support for Quad-led maritime initiatives and agreed to work towards developing a Common Operational Picture across the Indo-Pacific, strengthening regional maritime security and cooperation.

items.title: International arbitrators reject $134 million claim by Rwanda against U.K. for scrapped migrant deal
items.summary: An international arbitration panel has ruled in favour of the United Kingdom, rejecting Rwanda’s multimillion-pound claims linked to the scrapped migrant deportation agreement that was abandoned by Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government in 2024

items.title: Norway recovers porcelain, chandeliers from 18th century shipwreck
items.summary: Archaeologists in Norway have recovered Chinese porcelain, chandeliers and other artefacts from a newly discovered 18th-century shipwreck in the Skagerrak strait

items.title: UN labour agency freezes U.S. official’s appointment over unpaid dues
U.K. releases second batch of files on Mandelson's appointment as U.S. Ambassador
items.summary: The release resurfaces a saga that has weakened and embarrassed PM Starmer over the nine months since Bloomberg News exposed the extent of Mr. Mandelson’s ties with the late pedophile financier Jeffrey Epstein

items.title: Rescuers dig for bodies after a massive mining explosives blast in Myanmar kills at least 38
items.summary: Rescue workers in Myanmar used excavation machinery to recover bodies after a massive explosion involving stored mining explosives killed at least 38 people in Shan State near the Chinese border

items.title: French Navy, backed by U.K., intercepted sanctioned tanker from Russia: French President Emmanuel Macron
items.summary: France, with support from the U.K., intercepted a Russian-linked oil tanker in the Atlantic Ocean, stepping up efforts to enforce sanctions and disrupt the “shadow fleet” used by Moscow to sustain oil exports and finance its war in Ukraine

items.title: Israel orders strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs as Hezbollah rockets hit northern Israel
items.summary: Israel’s government has ordered strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut, as Hezbollah fired rockets at northern Israel including the outskirts of coastal city of Haifa

items.title: Rescuers search for alternative route to reach two missing in flooded Laos cave
items.summary: Rescue teams in Laos continued searching for 2 people believed to be trapped inside a flooded cave in Xaisomboun Province, nearly two weeks after heavy rainfall blocked the main entrance

items.title: India, Oman free trade pact comes into force
items.summary: Bilateral trade between India and Oman reached $11.18 billion in FY 2025-26, up from $10.61 billion in FY 2024-25

items.title: Hungary’s Prime Minister Péter Magyar to amend the Constitution to remove President Tamás Sulyok
items.summary: Newly appointed Prime Minister Péter Magyar says Hungary’s government will amend the Constitution to remove President Tamás Sulyok

items.title: China opposes ‘political suppression’ of Xinhua reporter in U.S.
items.summary: China accused the U.S. of politically suppressing a reporter from Xinhua News Agency, saying the journalist was working legally in the country

items.title: PM Modi holds talks with Myanmar President Aung Hlaing
items.summary: PM Narendra Modi held wide-ranging talks with Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing, focusing on trade, connectivity, border security and defence cooperation

items.title: Iran executes two men accused of leading early 2026 protests

items.title: Eight crested ibises released in Japan decades after extinction
items.summary: The birds have been raised at a conservation centre on Sado Island following a successful captive-breeding program and ten more birds are waiting to be released

items.title: Pro-Trump candidate pulls ahead in Colombia presidential vote as ruling party sows doubt in results
items.summary: Newcomer Aberaldo de la Espriella known as ‘El Tigre’ or ‘The Tiger’ is expected to scoop up support from voters who threw their support behind another conservative candidate in the first round

items.title: Explosion and fire at defence company in South Korea kills five
items.summary: The deadly incident happened at a worksite run by Hanwha Aerospace in the South Korean city of Daejeon

items.title: France says nearly 900 arrested in Champions League final riots
items.summary: One man died riding his motorbike around the Paris ring road in celebration while authorities reported stabbings and other attacks

items.title: Outgoing U.S. Federal Reserve chair Powell delivers defence of Fed independence
items.summary: Jerome Powell delivered a staunch defence of the need for the central bank to maintain independence and credibility, as it comes under assault from President Donald Trump
